utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: brittany edwards , 07 , 1898 . she is a return visit for ibs with functional abdominal pain , nausea , vomiting . ms. edwards is a pleasant , 32-year-old female who was last seen in august of 2019 with flares of abdominal pain , who was diagnosed with irritable bowl that was treated with bentyl .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: she had a previous prescription for reglan that she received from the emergency room that she used as needed for nausea . she was instructed to start a low dose fiber supplement , such as citrucel , daily , probiotics to help with gas and bloating , bentyl up to four times daily for intestinal cramping , and stop reglan and use zofran as needed . neuromodulators such as elavil and buspar were discussed but not started .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . how have you been feeling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not so good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , so my nurse told me you have been going out to eat some on friday nights and saturday nights and that you have had some spicy foods or spicy shrimp .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , your gut probably does not always appreciate that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , it does n't like it at all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , so ibs is one of those things where you are very sensitive to certain things such as spicy foods or fatty foods and alcohol . and this can cause lower belly stuff like cramps , or you can get upper belly stuff such as nausea .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been getting both .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , so how frequently have you been getting or having a bowel movement on normal days ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm starting to notice it's between two and three times a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are they soft stools or formed stools ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , they're formed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and after you have gone out either drinking with your friends , do you tend to have more diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you feel it's the food or the alcohol or both ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , well usually we go to friday's restaurant . i always eat pasta , the chicken alfredo .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i can s- i can bet it's the alfredo sauce .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but it normally does n't bother me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , pasta can make you bloated , uh , but it does n't usually cause diarrhea . grilled chi- uh , grilled chicken is usually all right . alfredo , though , is higher in fat , and it does contain dairy . but you're saying it usually does n't bother you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . what type of alcohol drink do you drink when you are there ? is it a sweet drink ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: most of the time it's either tequila or dark liquor . i prefer patron , but if i get brown liquor , i only get two . um , every now and again , i'll drink a martini .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sometimes it's the mixer they tend to put in the drinks , but it can be a variety of things . um , so when you're not eating out or drinking , what we do on a daily basis does help keep us regular , so when it does flare up , it c- it might not be as bad . um , have you had any luck using the fiber like citrucel regularly ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , no . i guess i need to find a new drink .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how is it during the week ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: like what i eat ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , and with the diarrhea and abdomal- um , abdominal pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right now , i'm still having the cramping and sharp pains , so i've been avoiding heavy foods . i did have some fried chicken today , though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and was everything all right after that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , for the most part .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . remember , it's also the portion sizes . so i mean , if you eat a little and you feel fine , then that's okay . you have to just really watch the portions .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good . i ca n't go out- go without some fried chicken at least once in a while .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sounds good . all right , well let's take a look at you today , and we'll do a brief physical exam . you will hear me call out some findings . i will answer any questions , and we will discuss once we have finished the exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so the patient is alert and cooperative , appears stated age . all right . i'm going to listen to your heart and lungs . and lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally . heart regular rate and rhythm . all right , so if you could just lay back for me , and i'll examine your abdomen .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . do you have any tenderness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not really .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so the abdomen is soft , non-tender . no masses or organomegaly . and let me take a listen . normal active bowel sounds . all right . you can go ahead and sit up now . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so first i want you to try citrucel . it does come in a tablet . you should use it twice daily for regularity . then i would say , if you know you're going out and will possibly have diarrhea as a result , you can go ahead and use your bentyl 20 milligrams . you can do one tablet before you eat , and then repeat up to four times daily . and then on days when you feel good , you do n't have to use anything . um , and then on friday before you leave the house , you can take a bentyl and put one in your pocket for later , and then you can repeat the same thing on saturday if you want , and that will be totally fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and so your bentyl , that's the anti-spasm medication , and i would take the higher dose .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: can i get a refill of that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , absolutely . uh , so take one tablet about 30 to 45 minutes before leaving the house and then the second tablet at the restaurant . then with the nausea , um , i think we should try and see if you can recognize a pattern with what you are eating and drinking , um , um , about , like , what makes it worse .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i noticed i've been doing better with drinking water instead of sodas .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . and then for your zofran , i would n't take that ahead of time . just plan on drinking plenty of water .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and you can take it , but before you do , we want to try to reduce the triggers . uh , so watch out for greasy or fatty foods and sugary drinks .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and so , however , if it is not just these isolated inci- incidents and the symptoms are happening all the time , we could put you on a long term medication that would take ... that you would take every night . and so then you do n't have to use so much bentyl or the zofran . how do you feel about that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was actually going to ask you about something like that to help avoid flares altogether .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , there is . and it actually works on the chemicals in your brain that are causing your gut to be overly sensitive . one is called elavil , but the only drawback is that it can make you a little sleepy . how does that sound ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that sounds like a much better plan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . we can definitely do that . uh , just take it every night before bedtime .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good . thank you so much .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . and so i have gone ahead and sent your prescriptions to your pharmacy . just give us a call if you have any questions or need us for anything else . and then let's plan on touching base through mychart email in about a month to just check in to see how you're doing with symptoms . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome , and have a good rest of your day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so review of symptoms is positive for diarrhea , nausea , and abdominal pain . assessment and plan . brittany edwards is a pleasant female with ibs and functional abdominal pain , aggravated by high stress , poor eating habits , and poor sleeping habits . i've encouraged dietary and lifestyle modifications , as well as starting a neuromodulator such as elavil 25 milligrams at bedtime for ibs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: she can use bentyl 20 milligrams as needed 30 to 45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant . she can also use zofran if needed . however , i have encouraged her to reduce greasy and fatty foods and alcohol consumption . i have asked her to follow up in one month by emailing over mychart regarding any side effects and issues , or issues with elavil or if adjustments are needed .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 32-year-old female returning for follow-up of IBS with functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
The patient was last seen in August 2019 for abdominal pain flares and was diagnosed with IBS.
Previous treatments included Reglan for nausea, fiber supplements, probiotics, and Bentyl for cramping.
The patient reports eating out on weekends and consuming spicy foods like shrimp.
IBS management
The doctor asks about the frequency of bowel movements.
The patient reports having two to three bowel movements per day.
The doctor asks about the consistency of the stools.
The patient reports that her stools are formed.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences diarrhea after drinking alcohol.
The patient confirms she experiences diarrhea after drinking alcohol.
The doctor asks if the symptoms are caused by food, alcohol, or both.
The patient reports eating chicken alfredo at a restaurant.
The patient consumes tequila or brown liquor, usually two drinks, and occasionally martinis.
The patient reports current cramping and sharp pains, and notes she ate fried chicken today.
Physical exam findings: lungs clear to auscultation bilaterally, heart regular rate and rhythm.
Physical exam findings: abdomen soft, non-tender, no masses or organomegaly, normoactive bowel sounds.
The doctor instructs the patient to take Citrucel twice daily and Bentyl 20mg as needed before eating out.
The patient asks for a refill of Bentyl.
The doctor confirms the refill and instructs the patient to take one Bentyl 30-45 minutes before leaving and one at the restaurant.
The doctor asks if the patient is interested in a long-term medication to prevent flares.
The doctor suggests Elavil as a neuromodulator for IBS, noting potential sleepiness as a side effect.
The doctor prescribes Elavil to be taken every night at bedtime.
The doctor instructs the patient to follow up via MyChart in one month.
